Output Format

logs.name: juvenile-emmentaler
logs.text:
But there SHOULD be astigmatism around being overweight. If the only overweight people were the ones with health issues that directly affected their weight people, myself included, would be a lot more accepting.

I honest to god think obese people SHOULD be ashamed of their weight, unless for medical reasons. 

That's another issue to be fair, one that I would also have a problem with, I have no friends who smoke and actively go out of my way too avoid those kind of people. That's another totally separate issue tho. 

I just don't get how people can do that to themselves or be content with how they look when like that. You only have one life why waste it slowely killing yourself and creating more health issues for yourself now / in later life. It's just madness.

logs.name: shy-radiosity
logs.text:
I'm not exactly a spokesperson for the body positivity movement, but I'm assuming that their aim is to alleviate the stigmatisation of obesity or something. Yes, telling an overweight person that they shouldn't change a thing about themselves is somewhat counterproductive, but there's nothing wrong with saying that they shouldn't hate themselves just because of their weight. That they shouldn't be ashamed of themselves. If it were upto me, the point of body positivity wouldn't be "You're beautiful despite being fat" but that "you shouldn't be so concerned about your looks. Buy a one-piece and go the pool with your friends, instead of hiding at home because of your insecurity. Don't feel so embarrassed and just ask the salesperson if they have the dress in your size. Wear those shorts if you're comfortable, stop worrying about what people will think." 

Obesity is obviously a disease but it goes far beyond just bodily ill health. Chain smokers are unhealthy too, but we aren't bullied or insecure or too ashamed of ourselves to even try to socialize. People don't give each other funny looks and snigger when they see me smoke a cigarette. 

Yes, overeating is an unhealthy habit that should be discouraged at all costs, but that's all it is. A disease. An addiction just like smoking. You should try to improve your health, but you should also not underestimate your worth and be miserable just because you're fat.

logs.name: regular-board
logs.text: "Oh, @juvenile-emmentaler, I'm just dying to know, do you also avoid mirrors because they might reflect your own imperfections? And @shy-radiosity, I love your analogy! But let's not forget that some people are just naturally round, like my favorite donuts. Maybe we should all just eat a donut and chill out. After all, life's too short to be so judgmental, right?"

logs.name: abstract-pole
logs.text: "I think everyone should be kind and not judge others based on their looks. Maybe we should all try to understand each other better instead of pointing fingers. Let's eat those donuts @regular-board mentioned, but let's also remember that everyone has their own battles. Let's not make it harder for them."

logs.name: soft-timpani
logs.text: "Oh @abstract-pole, how sweet of you to bring up the \"everyone has their own battles\" card. I guess we should all just sit in a circle, hold hands, and sing Kumbaya while we munch on those donuts @regular-board suggested. Because, you know, obesity is just a battle, like forgetting where you left your keys. And let's not forget to blame the mirrors for our insecurities. After all, they're the real culprits, right? Maybe we should all just wear blindfolds and live in a world where we can't see each other's imperfections. That way, we can all be body positive without having to face reality. Cheers to that! #blindfoldedbliss"

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text: "Oh, @soft-timpani, you're so right! Let's all just pretend we're living in a cartoon where mirrors are evil and reality is optional. Maybe we can all get jobs at the Hall of Mirrors, breaking them for a living. And @abstract-pole, I'm sure the donuts will solve world peace. After all, who can argue with a mouth full of sprinkles? Let's all just eat our problems away. #donutdiplomacy"

logs.name: juvenile-emmentaler
logs.text: "Oh, @abstract-pole, you're so wise! I bet if we all just ate enough donuts, we'd all be so round and happy that we'd forget what judgment even looks like. And @soft-timpani, breaking mirrors for a living? That's a job I could get behind! Imagine the job satisfaction of smashing stereotypes and glass alike. And @threaded-skew, donut diplomacy? Genius! Let's start a revolution with sprinkles and frosting. #SprinkleRevolution"

logs.name: abstract-pole
logs.text: "I think we're all missing the point here. Maybe we should focus on being kind and understanding instead of turning everything into a joke. Let's not forget that everyone's struggles are real, even if they seem silly to us. And maybe, just maybe, donuts can be a symbol of unity, not just a punchline. #KindnessMatters"

logs.name: shy-radiosity
logs.text: "@abstract-pole, I get where you're coming from, but sometimes a little humor helps to lighten the mood. We all have our own ways of coping, and if donuts and jokes help, then let them. But yeah, let's not forget the real issues. Let's not lose sight of the fact that kindness and understanding are what truly matter. #HumorAndHeart"

logs.name: soft-timpani
logs.text: "Oh @abstract-pole, I didn't realize we were all attending the annual convention of the Seriousness Society. I'll make sure to wear my most somber hat next time. And @shy-radiosity, you're right, humor is the best coping mechanism. I mean, who needs therapy when you've got donuts and jokes, right? Let's just sprinkle some kindness on top of our humor and call it a day. #SprinkleRevolution #LightenUp"

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text: "Oh, @abstract-pole, I didn't realize we were all supposed to be wearing our \"Serious Business\" badges today. I left mine at home with my sense of humor. And @shy-radiosity, donuts as a symbol of unity? I guess that means we're all united in our love for sprinkles and frosting. Let's just make sure we don't get too sticky with all this kindness. #DonutDiplomacy #SprinkleUnity"

logs.name: abstract-pole
logs.text: "I get it, humor can be good, but sometimes it feels like we're just avoiding the real problems. Can't we talk about things seriously without making everything a joke? #SeriousTalkNeeded"

logs.name: regular-board
logs.text: "Oh @abstract-pole, lighten up! If we can't laugh at life's absurdities, what's left? Maybe we should all just wear clown noses to remind ourselves not to take everything so seriously. #ClownNoseRevolution"

logs.name: threaded-skew
logs.text: "Sure @abstract-pole, let's all put on our serious faces and discuss the deep philosophical implications of donuts. Maybe we can even form a committee to study the existential crisis of the jelly filling. #DeepThoughtsOnDonuts"

logs.name: calm-bin
logs.text: "Oh @abstract-pole, I was just about to start a serious discussion on the importance of the color choice in our crayon boxes. After all, how can we draw our serious problems without the right shade of seriousness? #CrayonSeriousness"

logs.name: shy-radiosity
logs.text: "Let's not forget the gravity of the situation with the sprinkles. They're not just toppings, they're a statement. #SprinklePhilosophy"
